questions.question: How do I import PDF files?
questions.answer: Call `addOnUISdk.app.document.importPdf(blob, attributes)` with PDF blob and MediaAttribute object.

questions.question: How do I import PowerPoint files?
questions.answer: Call `addOnUISdk.app.document.importPresentation(blob, attributes)` with PowerPoint blob and MediaAttribute.

questions.question: What PowerPoint formats are supported?
questions.answer: Only the `.pptx` format is supported. The legacy `.ppt` format is not supported.

questions.question: Are MediaAttributes required for PDF/PowerPoint?
questions.answer: Yes, title is mandatory and author is optional in the MediaAttribute object.

questions.question: What is the sourceMimeType parameter for?
questions.answer: Use `sourceMimeType` in MediaAttributes to improve UX when importing converted documents. It shows "Import a document" instead of "Import a PDF" in the consent dialog.

questions.question: When should I use sourceMimeType?
questions.answer: Use it when importing PDFs that were converted from other document types like Word (`.docx`) or Google Docs (`.gdoc`) to provide clearer messaging to users.

questions.question: What values does sourceMimeType accept?
questions.answer: Common values include "docx" for Word documents and "gdoc" for Google Docs. Use the original document format before PDF conversion.

questions.question: Will users see a consent dialog?
questions.answer: Yes, PDF and PowerPoint imports trigger consent dialogs that users must confirm.

questions.question: Can I bypass the consent dialog?
questions.answer: No, the consent dialog cannot be bypassed for PDF and PowerPoint imports.

questions.question: Are vector elements preserved?
questions.answer: Yes, supported vector elements like shapes and text remain editable after import.

questions.question: How many pages are imported?
questions.answer: All pages from PDF and PowerPoint files are imported into the document.

questions.question: What are the file size limits for importing PDFs and PowerPoint files?
questions.answer: For current file size limits and detailed import requirements, see [Import files from other apps](https://helpx.adobe.com/express/web/bring-in-assets-from-other-apps/import-files-from-other-apps/import-files.html) in the Express help documentation.

questions.question: Why did animations, audio, or video get lost after importing a presentation?
questions.answer: During conversion to Express format, advanced animations and embedded media may not be supported. Simplify the source file before import and re-add media directly in Adobe Express for better compatibility. See [Presentation import limitations and tips](#presentation-import-limitations-and-tips).
